description: Fix bugs, resource leaks, and code quality issues in the Chrome extension — background service worker, offscreen document, network capture, and side panel React code.

userStories.title: Add error handling to onInstalled/onStartup lifecycle handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the extension lifecycle handlers to log errors so that silent failures during extension startup are diagnosable.
acceptanceCriteria: The onInstalled and onStartup handlers in background.ts have .catch() handlers that log warnings, matching the pattern used by the top-level calls on lines 129-131
If ensureOffscreenDocument() fails inside onInstalled, setupKeepaliveAlarm() and reinjectStoredPlugins() still do not execute (sequential dependency is correct), but the error is logged
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts lines 113-127. The `void (async () => { ... })()` pattern silently swallows errors. Add a `.catch((err: unknown) => console.warn(...))` to each IIFE, matching the pattern on lines 129-131. The simplest fix is to add `.catch(...)` to the end of each IIFE call. Keep the `void` prefix since the return value is intentionally discarded.

userStories.title: Add port range validation for storage change relay
userStories.description: As a user, I want invalid port numbers to be rejected so that out-of-range or fractional port values don't produce invalid WebSocket URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: The port validation in background.ts checks that the port is an integer AND within the range 1-65535
A port value of 99999, 0.5, or -1 does NOT trigger a ws:setUrl message
A port value of 9515, 1, or 65535 still triggers a ws:setUrl message correctly
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts line 141. Currently checks `typeof portChange?.newValue === 'number' && portChange.newValue > 0`. Change to also check `Number.isInteger(portChange.newValue) && portChange.newValue <= 65535`.

userStories.title: Clean up stale window IDs in side panel toggle on window removal
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel toggle to work correctly after closing and reopening Chrome windows, without needing to click twice.
acceptanceCriteria: A chrome.windows.onRemoved listener is registered in initSidePanelToggle that removes the closed window ID from the openWindows set
The listener is only registered when canToggle is true (Chrome 141+)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel-toggle.ts. The `openWindows` Set accumulates stale IDs when windows close while the side panel is open (onClosed may not fire). Add `chrome.windows.onRemoved.addListener((windowId) => { openWindows.delete(windowId); });` inside the `if (canToggle)` block.

userStories.title: Clear stale lastDisconnectReason in disconnectAndReconnect
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the correct error state in the side panel when the port changes or a force reconnect is triggered, instead of a flash of the previous error.
acceptanceCriteria: disconnectAndReconnect in offscreen/index.ts clears lastDisconnectReason to undefined before closing the WebSocket
After a port change, the side panel does not briefly show 'Authentication Failed' if the previous disconnect was auth_failed
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts line 122-141. The `disconnectAndReconnect` function resets `backoffMs` but does not clear `lastDisconnectReason`. Add `lastDisconnectReason = undefined;` at the top of the function body (after `backoffMs = INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS`).

userStories.title: Add pendingRequests cleanup for aborted network requests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the network capture pendingRequests map to not grow unboundedly when requests are aborted without CDP events.
acceptanceCriteria: pendingRequests entries older than a reasonable TTL (e.g., 60 seconds) are pruned during new requestWillBeSent events or periodically
The pruning does not affect requests that are still actively loading (only stale entries)
requestIdToRequest entries for requests that never receive loadingFinished are also cleaned up
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts. The `pendingRequests` Map (line 76) and `requestIdToRequest` Map (line 78) can leak entries for requests that never complete. Add a TTL-based cleanup: during `Network.requestWillBeSent` handling, before adding the new entry, prune any pendingRequests entries older than 60 seconds. Similarly, prune requestIdToRequest entries that reference requests no longer in the requests array. A simple approach: add a `const PENDING_REQUEST_TTL_MS = 60_000;` constant, and at the start of the requestWillBeSent handler, iterate pendingRequests and delete entries where `Date.now() - entry.timestamp > PENDING_REQUEST_TTL_MS`.

userStories.title: Prune stale keys from rate limiter methodTimestamps map
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the rate limiter to not accumulate empty timestamp arrays for methods that are no longer being called.
acceptanceCriteria: After pruning expired timestamps, if the resulting array is empty, the key is deleted from the methodTimestamps map
The checkRateLimit function still correctly tracks and limits active methods
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/rate-limiter.ts lines 54-63. After the filter on line 54 (`timestamps = ... .filter(t => t > cutoff)`), if timestamps is empty and the request is also rejected (won't happen — empty means allowed), or more importantly: when timestamps become empty after filtering, the key is still set back into the map on line 57 or 62. Fix: after the filter, if `timestamps.length === 0` and the request is not about to be added, delete the key. The simplest approach: after line 54, if `timestamps.length === 0`, skip the `methodTimestamps.set` on line 57 (the reject path won't be reached since 0 < maxRequests). On line 62, the new timestamp is always pushed so the array is always non-empty in the allow path. The issue is specifically on the reject path (line 57-58): if all timestamps expired but the count check somehow fails — actually this cannot happen since 0 < maxRequests. The real leak is that keys with expired-but-filtered timestamps are set back. After line 54, add: `if (timestamps.length === 0) { methodTimestamps.delete(method); }` — but then immediately re-add on line 62. Better approach: only set the map key on line 62 (allow path). Move `methodTimestamps.set(method, timestamps)` from line 57 to be only in the reject path, and in the allow path (line 62) always set. And delete empty keys in the reject path.

userStories.title: Clear CountdownBar interval when countdown reaches zero
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CountdownBar component to stop its interval timer when the countdown reaches zero, avoiding unnecessary CPU work.
acceptanceCriteria: The setInterval in CountdownBar is cleared when remaining reaches 0
The countdown bar still visually shows 0s at the end
No interval continues ticking after the countdown expires
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx lines 28-36. The interval continues firing after remaining reaches 0. Inside the `update` function, check if the computed remaining is 0 and clear the interval if so. Store the interval ID in a variable accessible to the update function (it already is via `id`): after `setRemaining(Math.max(0, ...))`, add `if (timeoutMs - elapsed <= 0) clearInterval(id);`.

userStories.title: Clear toolFilter on disconnect in side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool filter to be cleared when the server disconnects so that reconnecting shows all plugins without a stale filter hiding some.
acceptanceCriteria: When sp:connectionState fires with connected=false, toolFilter is set to ''
After reconnecting, all plugins are visible (no stale filter)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx lines 110-116. The disconnect handler clears plugins, failedPlugins, activeTools, and pendingConfirmations — but not toolFilter. Add `setToolFilter('');` alongside the other state resets in the `else` branch (after `setPendingConfirmations([])`).

userStories.title: Add error handling to getConnectionState in side panel startup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel to handle Chrome runtime errors during startup gracefully instead of getting stuck in the loading state.
acceptanceCriteria: The getConnectionState() call in the initial useEffect has a .catch() handler
If getConnectionState() rejects, loading is set to false and connected defaults to false
The side panel shows the disconnected state instead of infinite loading
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx lines 74-82. The `void getConnectionState().then(...)` has no `.catch()`. Add `.catch(() => { setLoading(false); })` after the `.then()` call. This ensures the side panel exits the loading state even if the Chrome runtime is unavailable.

userStories.title: Fix optimistic revert race condition in handleToggleTool
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool toggle state to remain consistent with the server even under rapid toggling with server errors.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToggleTool captures the original tools array before the optimistic update (matching the pattern in handleToggleAll)
The .catch() handler reverts to the captured original state instead of toggling back
Rapid toggling with server errors does not desynchronize the displayed state from the server state
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx lines 53-60. Currently the catch handler reverts by toggling `!newEnabled`, which is wrong under rapid toggling. The fix: capture `const originalTools = plugin.tools;` before the optimistic update (like handleToggleAll does on line 45), then in the catch handler use `updatePluginTools(() => originalTools)`. This matches the safe pattern already in the codebase on lines 44-51.
